title: How Scientists Found Clues to Make Gold in Space
summary: Scientists have solved a big mystery about how gold is made in space. Gold forms when stars explode or crash together. This process is called the r-process, where atoms grab many neutrons quickly, become unstable, and then break apart. A team from the University of Tennessee worked with other scientists at CERN in Europe to study this. They used a rare atom called indium-134 and watched it decay into tin atoms. They discovered how two neutrons are released during this decay, which helps us understand how gold is created. This research can help make better models of stars and predict how strange atoms behave. It's like finding a missing piece in a puzzle about space and elements.

background_read: Gold is a precious metal used in jewelry and electronics. It doesn't form on Earth naturally but is created in space during big events like star explosions. Scientists study this to understand how all heavy elements, like gold and platinum, are made. They use special labs and atoms to learn more about these space processes, which helps us know more about the universe and how it works.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists from the University of Tennessee and CERN
WHAT: They studied how gold forms in space by watching atoms decay
WHY: To solve a mystery about gold creation and improve star models

perspectives.description: This research helps us learn how elements like gold are made in the universe.
